name: Superblocks Internal Tools API
what_it_does: Enterprise internal tools platform with REST API for building admin panels, workflows, and automation — supporting 50+ data source integrations (databases, APIs, SaaS), Python/JavaScript code blocks, scheduled jobs, and agent-triggered workflows.
use_cases: Triggering Superblocks workflows programmatically via REST API from external agents
Scheduling and managing automated data workflows across connected data sources
Building internal admin tools that call external APIs through Superblocks integrations
Executing Python and JavaScript code blocks as serverless functions via API
Monitoring workflow execution status and logs for automation observability
not_for: Customer-facing product UIs — Superblocks is for internal tools only
High-throughput data processing pipelines (use Airflow or Prefect)
best_when: An agent needs to trigger pre-built internal workflows that operate on databases and SaaS tools, without rebuilding integrations from scratch.
avoid_when: You need a customer-facing interface or high-throughput data pipeline — Superblocks targets internal tooling for operations teams.

auth.notes: API keys for external API triggers. SSO/SAML for user authentication. Role-based access control for resource permissions. Agent-level API keys for workflow execution.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free tier for small teams (limited apps and users)
pricing.notes: Per-seat pricing model. Enterprise features include on-premises deployment, audit logs, and advanced security controls. Competitive with Retool for enterprise internal tools.

performance.rate_limits: Not publicly documented; enterprise SLAs available

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Developer documentation covers workflow triggering via API. Python and JavaScript execution environments well-documented. Error responses include execution logs. Workflow execution is async — status polling required.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Workflow triggers are not inherently idempotent — duplicate triggers run workflow multiple times. Implement client-side deduplication for critical workflows.
known_agent_gotchas: Workflow execution is async — agents must poll for completion status
Data source integration credentials managed in Superblocks UI — not via API
Code block environments have execution time limits — long-running workflows need chunking
Enterprise features (on-prem) significantly different from cloud API behavior

security_score_components.security_notes: SOC2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. Role-based access control. TLS enforced. Secrets management for data source credentials. On-prem deployment option for high-security environments. SSO/SAML for enterprise auth.
